target.name: Netrasemi
target.founder: Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, Sreejith Varma

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Netrasemi is a Series A stage semiconductor company founded by Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, and Sreejith Varma. The company has raised ₹107 Cr (~$13M) to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-stage, same-sector private peers in Chips & Semiconductors, supplemented by reference to 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach. Four Series A stage chip companies—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—establish a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (median 22.3x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure, typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), centered on $1.5B median. This reflects the peer-median method's result with reasonable downside and upside bounds. Key uncertainties include Netrasemi's unreported revenue trajectory, the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($500M–$5.0B), and the limited public comparability in a sector where private rounds increasingly deviate from public multiples. The $13M raised-to-date implies a seed or early-stage valuation substantially below this Series A range, suggesting material step-up if the median holds.
